methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $25M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Robotics & Manufacturing (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 11 same-sector peers exist in Robotics & Manufacturing, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Persona AI is a seed-stage robotics and manufacturing company founded by Nic Radford and Jerry Pratt that has raised $25M to date. The robotics and manufacturing sector contains a broad peer set of nine private companies and one public company (277810.KQ), including later-stage players such as 1X Technologies, Divergent, Exotec, Locus Robotics, and Bedrock Robotics. However, the peer set lacks sufficient same-stage or adjacent-stage comparables to support a reliable peer-median approach—only one comparable yielded sufficient data density for triangulation.

Given this constraint, the Stage-Progression method is the primary applicable approach. Using the $25M total raised as a valuation proxy and applying a 2.5x step-up multiplier typical of Seed-to-Series A progression yields an implied valuation of $62M. This method assumes Persona AI's next financing round would follow standard stage-advancement conventions in venture capital.

The analysis supports a range of $53M to $72M (median $62M), with the estimate centered on the single stage-progression signal. The primary limitation is methodological: absence of tight seed-stage peers in robotics manufacturing constrains triangulation to one method. Key uncertainties include the timing and terms of the next funding round, relative product maturity versus the named comparables, and whether the 2.5x multiplier appropriately reflects market conditions at Persona AI's next milestone. Additional comparable data or market-rate financing terms would refine this estimate materially.
